_core_paths_doc: BLUE crates per CLAUDE.md. ade_runtime is RED (shell); ade_testkit and ade_node are neither. ade_network has per-submodule BLUE scoping: mux::frame is BLUE; mux::transport and session are RED. ade_core::consensus (S-B1 onward) is BLUE under the already-listed crate prefix. Substring matches against absolute file paths.
_replay_cmd_doc: ade_testkit hosts boundary_replay, differential_*_replay, ledger_determinism, and stateful_replay test suites.
_replay_on_stop_doc: Disabled by default. Phase 4 test runtime is acceptable but full replay across all eras is not sub-second; flip to true once selective replay is introduced.
_clusters_dir_doc: Canonical IDD location. Currently contains PHASE4-N-D (in-flight). Closed Phase 0A/0B/1/2A/2C clusters remain at docs/completed/phase_*/ as historical anchors.
_cluster_id_format_doc: Cluster IDs use the PHASE<N>-<LETTER> convention (e.g., PHASE4-N-A, PHASE4-N-D). Slash commands pass the cluster id as the literal directory name under docs/clusters/.
_clusters_archive_dir_doc: Where /cluster-close moves cluster docs upon closure. Created on demand.
_planning_doc_doc: Active phase plan. /cluster-doc reads this when seeding new cluster docs (N-A, N-B, N-C, N-E, N-F).
_grounding_docs_dir_doc: Where the four grounding docs live: ade-CODEMAP.md, ade-SEAMS.md, ade-HEAD_DELTAS.md, ade-TRACEABILITY.md.

_normative_docs_doc: Files /traceability reads to extract rules. Tier-gate statement and its Tier 5 addendum carry the most enforced rules; the contract docs anchor cross-surface invariants; CLAUDE.md carries the commit-attribution policy.

_invariant_registry_doc: Canonical, append-only invariant registry. Schema follows ziranity-v3's constitution_rule_registry.toml (base fields identical), additively compatible with the IDD template. 359 entries at HEAD (225 enforced / 19 partial / 114 declared / 1 enforced_scaffolding / 0 deprecated) across families T (true), CN (constraint network), DC (derived constraint), OP (operational), RO (release obligation). Live: run-4 CE-AH-6 -- cert-free single-producer block production on C2-LOCAL vs a real cardano-node 11.0.1 relay, NOT preprod. Prior through PHASE4-N-AE.F (enforced): DC-NODE-16 (receive idempotency at the durable-admit chokepoint -- a peer-delivered block already durably present byte-identically in the ChainDb, same slot AND same hash, is an idempotent no-op at pump_block: no validation step, no WAL append, no tip change, replay-equivalent; a DIFFERENT block, different hash, at/before the last-applied slot is NOT short-circuited and reaches the unchanged BLUE header authority and fails closed; gated on HASH equality vs the durable store, never slot alone; closes the post-CE-A5 echo where the relay re-announced Ade's own adopted tip and the BLUE authority rejected SlotBeforeLastApplied, ending an otherwise-successful continuous run with exit 43 AFTER AddedToCurrentChain; RED chokepoint only, NO BLUE change). CN-CONS-06 first live cross-impl acceptance, DC-WAL-02/T-REC-05 recover->follow continuity). The CE-A5 manifest: a real cardano-node 11.0.1 relay AddedToCurrentChain an Ade-forged block. Prior through PHASE4-N-AC (enforced): DC-CRYPTO-10 (the RED producer signing shell evolves the operator KES key forward to the requested KES period before signing -- kes_sign_header_advancing = kes_advance_to(period) then kes_sign_header, reusing the existing deterministic Sum6KES update; fail-closed EvolutionBackwards backwards / EvolutionExhausted beyond the key lifetime; the forge's single real KES sign is rewired to it so the forge works across KES periods, not just the minted period 0) + strengthening CN-KES-HEADER-01 (the header KES signature is over the canonical unsigned-header pre-image AND is now signed with the key evolved to the block's KES period). Prior through PHASE4-N-AB (enforced): CN-SESS-05 (outbound mux segmentation -- the GREEN session reducer segments outbound mini-protocol payloads > MAX_PAYLOAD into ordered <=MAX_PAYLOAD mux frames via the single encode_inner_frame authority and fails closed above the fixed non-configurable MAX_OUTBOUND_PAYLOAD_BYTES = 16 MiB; the outbound inverse of CN-SESS-04 inbound reassembly, so Ade can SERVE a >64 KB block) + strengthenings CN-SESS-04 + DC-SERVEMEM-01 (inbound reassembly now has a symmetric outbound segmentation; the bounded peer-driven serve range can now transmit large blocks within the bound). Prior through PHASE4-N-AA (enforced): DC-SERVEMEM-01 (bounded peer-driven serve range -- the --mode node serve caps each peer request at MAX_SERVE_RANGE_BLOCKS=256 via hash-free bounded ChainDb primitives + fail-closed; closes the PHASE4-N-U cross-slice security MEDIUM; serve-side analog of DC-LIVEMEM-01) + strengthenings DC-NODE-13 + DC-LIVEMEM-01 (durable-chain serve projection now bounded; receive-side bound now symmetric on the serve side). Prior through PHASE4-N-U (all enforced): DC-NODE-12 + DC-CONS-23 + DC-WAL-04 + T-REC-05 + DC-NODE-13 (forged-block durability -- own-forged durable admit via pump_block, forged-tip warm-start forward-replay recovery, serve-as-durable-chain projection) + strengthenings CN-CONS-07 + DC-NODE-11 (serve-provenance / monotone-gate superseded). Prior through PHASE4-N-F-G-R: DC-NODE-09 (G-K, serve-listener lifetime), CN-WIRE-10 (G-L, serve-side N2N handshake V15 cardano-node compat -- live-confirmed), CN-WIRE-11 (G-M, real-node ChainSync FindIntersect + ArrayHead), T-REC-04 + DC-CINPUT-03 (G-N, WarmStart eta0 seed-epoch sidecar), CN-WIRE-12 (G-O, feed-side BlockFetch tag-24 unwrap), DC-CINPUT-04 (G-P, feed header-validation view), DC-NODE-10 (G-Q, forge-successor evolved-spine position), DC-NODE-11 (G-R, monotone served-chain stability gate).
_ci_dirs_doc: CI scripts live as `ci/ci_check_*.sh` (ci_dirs = ["ci"]). A non-gating .github/workflows/notify-atlas.yml dispatch workflow also exists (grounding-doc -> ade-atlas rebuild trigger), but it is not an Ade invariant gate; add '.github/workflows' to ci_dirs only if/when an actual invariant gate is added there.
